round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent strengthened its case by integrating the '012' area code evidence from the regulatory/meta context, moving from a general Dutch identification to a specific Belgian regional identification.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent improved by synthesizing the linguistic evidence (area code) with its visual observations of brickwork and terrain to rule out the Netherlands.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent improved by using the linguistic/regulatory evidence to contextualize the botanical observations, moving from a general 'Low Countries' guess to a specific Belgian one.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent improved by incorporating the specific phone number data to resolve the ambiguity between Belgian and Dutch infrastructure standards.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent improved by using the linguistic evidence to confirm the specific regional style of the utility poles and architecture.

role_leakage_notes.landscape: Minor leakage: Referenced the 'Belgian area code' (regulatory/linguistic) and 'utility pole designs' (meta/regulatory) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Referenced the '012 area code' (regulatory) and 'Dutch text' (linguistic) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Referenced the 'Belgian area code' (linguistic/regulatory) and 'utility poles' (meta) in R2.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Minor leakage: Referenced the '012 area code' (linguistic/regulatory) in R2.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the '012' area code as the decisive evidence for Belgium (Turnhout/Antwerp region). It effectively synthesized the linguistic, landscape, and meta evidence to rule out the Netherlands, providing a clear and accurate chain of reasoning.
